--- a/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex
+++ b/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex
@@ -4371,10 +4371,19 @@ linear algebra), we package the construction here.
 \section{Representability of the Quot scheme}
 \label{sec:quot_representable}
 
-\begin{theorem}\leanok
+\begin{theorem}\notready
 [Representability of the Quot functor]
   \label{thm:quot_representable}
   \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme}
+  % LEAN/BLUEPRINT MISMATCH (see inbox I-0118): this statement carries the
+  % correct Nitsure hypotheses (\(\pi\) projective, \(L\) relatively very ample,
+  % \(E\) coherent).  The linked Lean \texttt{QuotScheme} instead hypothesizes
+  % only properness + locally-finite-type over arbitrary quasi-coherent \(L,E\)
+  % --- a strictly weaker, FALSE-as-pinned signature (Hironaka smooth-proper
+  % non-projective counterexample family; the functor is only an algebraic
+  % space).  Mathlib v4.31 lacks projectivity/very-ampleness vocabulary, so the
+  % Lean cannot yet faithfully formalize this statement; hence \notready, not
+  % \leanok, until the Lean signature is restated with projectivity.
   \uses{def:quot_functor, lem:quot_boundedness, lem:quot_alpha_injective,
     lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W, lem:quot_valuative_criterion,
     thm:grassmannian_representable, thm:flattening_stratification_exists,
